input.initialPrompt:
I have an HTTP-based C# MCP server built with ASP.NET Core.
How do I write integration tests that test the full MCP protocol
over HTTP without spinning up a real server?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to write integration tests for a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server built with ASP.NET Core, testing the full MCP protocol over HTTP without spinning up a real server. This is a knowledge/guidance question, so I can answer directly without needing to explore a codebase.

The standard approach for integration testing ASP.NET Core applications without a real server is to use `WebApplicationFactory<T>` from `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing`, which creates an in-memory test server. Combined with the MCP protocol (which uses JSON-RPC over HTTP with SSE for streaming), you can write tests that exercise the full protocol stack.


I'll walk through a practical example showing how to set up the test factory, create test clients that send MCP requests, and verify responses—covering both standard JSON-RPC calls and SSE streaming scenarios.
